steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: The angles $x^{\circ}$ and $133^{\circ}$ are adjacent angles formed where transversal $t$ intersects line $j$. Since they share a common side and together form a straight angle on line $j$, they form a linear pair.

steps.title: Apply the supplementary angle property
steps.content:
Angles that form a linear pair are supplementary, meaning they sum to $180^{\circ}$.
$$x + 133 = 180$$
$$x = 47$$
